title: 10 Herbal Creams For Gum Infection
intro_desc: Herbal creams for gum infection have been gaining popularity as a natural remedy to alleviate symptoms and promote healing. One of the key herbs responsible for this relief is Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil. This essential oil contains compounds that have antibacterial properties, which help to combat the underlying infection that's causing the gum inflammation. When applied topically as a cream, tea tree oil can reduce swelling, ease pain, and prevent further infection.  Another herb that's often used to treat gum infections is Zingiber officinale, commonly known as ginger. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the affected area, reducing discomfort and pain. It also has antimicrobial properties that can help to prevent the spread of infection and promote healing.  Echinacea purpurea is another herb that's commonly used to treat gum infections. It's known for its immune-boosting properties, which can help to fight off the underlying infection and promote overall oral health. When taken as a supplement or applied topically as a cream, Echinacea purpurea can help to reduce inflammation, ease pain, and prevent further infection.  When used together, these herbal creams can provide a natural and effective way to treat gum infections. They can help to reduce symptoms, promote healing, and prevent further complications. By using herbal creams, you can avoid the harsh chemicals and antibiotics found in traditional treatments, and instead opt for a more natural and gentle approach to oral health. This can bring numerous benefits, including reduced inflammation, eased pain, and improved overall oral health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains bioactive constituents like cineole, terpinen-4-ol, and limonene. These compounds have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial growth in gum infections. Cineole and terpinen-4-ol also have anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the infected area. Additionally, the antioxidants in Melaleuca alternifolia creams help protect the gums from further damage caused by free radicals. By using these creams, the antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties can help promote healing and reduce the severity of gum infections.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for gum infection
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, and a small pot for melting.
Melt beeswax and coconut oil in the small pot over low heat. Stir until fully melted.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Remove the pot from heat and let it cool slightly. Add distilled water and stir until combined.
Add Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil and stir well. Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in gum infections. These compounds also have antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections. The phenolic compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have antioxidant properties that help protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of Zingiber officinale creams help to reduce the severity of gum infections and promote healing. The combination of these properties makes Zingiber officinale creams a potentially effective treatment for gum infections.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for gum infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 1 cup of coconut oil.
Melt the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Add 1 tablespoon of grated fresh ginger root to the melted coconut oil and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and then add the distilled water and 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the mixture.
Whisk the mixture until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture has cooled and thickened.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains active constituents like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea also have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat the bacteria that cause gum infections. Additionally, the rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid have antioxidant properties that can protect the gums from damage caused by free radicals. This combination of properties makes Echinacea purpurea creams a potential treatment for gum infections.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for gum infection
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of aloe vera gel, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler. Heat until beeswax is fully melted.
Add aloe vera gel, vitamin E oil, and dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Remove from heat and let it cool for 10-15 minutes. Stir occasionally to prevent solidification.
Once cooled, pour the mixture into small containers. Let it cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains curcumin, a bioactive compound that has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Curcumin targets the root cause of gum infections by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing inflammation in the gums. The curcumin in Curcuma longa creams also has antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals and promote healing in the affected area. Additionally, curcumin has been shown to have antimicrobial activity against a range of bacteria, including those that cause gum infections such as Streptococcus mutans and Porphyromonas gingivalis. By reducing inflammation and killing bacteria, Curcuma longa creams can help to alleviate symptoms of gum infection and promote oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for gum infection
remedy_procedure: Mix 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (Curcuma longa) with 1 cup of coconut oil in a small bowl.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 30 seconds to melt the ingredients.
Remove from heat and stir in 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and 2 tablespoons of essential oil (optional).
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify before applying to gums with infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams contains tannins, ursolic acid, and triterpenoids, which are anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. These compounds help reduce swelling and fight off bacteria that cause gum infections. The antimicrobial properties of ursolic acid and triterpenoids inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Streptococcus and Escherichia, which are commonly associated with gum infections. Tannins also help to reduce bleeding and inflammation in the gums by constricting blood vessels and reducing the release of histamine. By applying Salvia officinalis creams to the affected area, the antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties can help to heal and protect the gums from infection.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for gum infection
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of dried Salvia officinalis leaves, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil.
Dry and crush the Salvia officinalis leaves in a mortar and pestle to make a fine powder.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat.
Add the crushed Salvia officinalis powder and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once set, your Salvia officinalis cream is ready to use for gum infection.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains eucalyptol, a key bioactive constituent that has antimicrobial properties. Eucalyptol works by killing bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus and Escherichia, thereby reducing inflammation and preventing further damage. Eucalyptol also has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections. Additionally, eucalyptol's ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria helps to prevent the spread of infection, promoting a faster healing process. This makes Eucalyptus globulus creams a useful treatment option for gum infections.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for gum infection
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of eucalyptus oil, and 2 tablespoons of peppermint oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of boiling water.
Once melted, remove from heat and add the shea butter. Stir until fully incorporated.
Add the eucalyptus oil and peppermint oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify before use. Apply to affected area to help soothe gum infections.

remedies.remedy_desc: Syzygium aromaticum creams contains active constituents like caryophyllene, eugenol, and limonene. These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat gum infections. Caryophyllene and eugenol have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis. Limonene has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the gums. Eugenol also has analgesic properties that help alleviate discomfort and pain associated with gum infections.
remedies.image_alt: Syzygium aromaticum herbal creams for gum infection
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Syzygium aromaticum essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of simmering water.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully dissolved.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly before adding the Syzygium aromaticum essential oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and solidify completely. Use as a cream for gum infection after it's solidified.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol, carvacrol, and borneol as its active constituents. These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help combat gum infections. Thymol, in particular, is effective against bacteria that cause infections, while carvacrol reduces inflammation and pain. Borneol, on the other hand, helps to heal and protect the gums from further damage. By using Thymus vulgaris creams, you can target the root cause of gum infections and promote a healthy oral environment.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for gum infection
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1/4 cup of dried thyme leaves in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat and add the thyme leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl. Discard the solids.
Let the liquid cool to room temperature. Then, mix 1/4 cup of coconut oil or shea butter with the thyme liquid.
Pour the mixture into a small container and store it in the fridge. Use a small amount on the affected gum area 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and triterpene saponins, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling and fight off bacterial infections that cause gum inflammation. The sesquiterpene lactones, particularly calendulic acid, inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that contribute to gum infections. The flavonoids and triterpene saponins in Calendula officinalis creams also have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the gums from oxidative stress and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Calendula officinalis creams can help to alleviate symptoms of gum infection and promote a healthy oral environment.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for gum infection
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Heat coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until beeswax melts.
Add Calendula officinalis flowers to the oil mixture and let it steep for 2 hours or overnight. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl.
Combine the strained oil mixture with shea butter and vitamin E oil in a bowl. Stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, use it as a cream to soothe gum infections.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These constituents have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help fight infections and reduce swelling. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria that can cause gum infections. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams also have antioxidant properties, which help protect the gums from further damage. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help promote healing and prevent further complications in gum infections.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for gum infection
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Distilled Water, 2 tablespoons of Glycerin, 2 tablespoons of Glycyrrhiza glabra root extract, 2 tablespoons of Beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Coconut Oil, and 2 teaspoons of Vitamin E oil.
Mix 1 cup of Distilled Water and 2 tablespoons of Glycerin in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat until the Glycerin dissolves.
Add 2 tablespoons of Glycyrrhiza glabra root extract and 2 teaspoons of Vitamin E oil to the saucepan. Stir well to combine.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool slightly. Add 2 tablespoons of Beeswax and 2 tablespoons of Coconut Oil to the mixture. Stir until the Beeswax dissolves.
Pour the mixture into a small container. Let it cool and solidify completely. Once solid, your Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is ready to use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent gum infection by reducing inflammation and killing bacteria. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have antibacterial properties that can help keep your mouth clean. They may also ease sore gums and reduce swelling, making it easier to maintain good oral hygiene and prevent infection.
faq_safe: Herbal teas may help with gum infection, but drinking them every day can be a problem. Some herbs can be too strong and cause more harm than good. The risks are higher if you have other health issues or take medications. Be cautious and consider your health before making it a daily habit.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with gum infections, but it's not a quick fix. It can take a few days to a week to notice improvement. Some people might see changes in their gums within 2-3 days, but it's normal for the healing process to take longer. The length of time depends on the severity of the infection.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for a gum infection is best in the morning or early afternoon. These times allow the tea's antibacterial properties to take effect throughout the day, helping to combat infection and reduce inflammation. This can help your gums feel better and support the healing process.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for gum infections. This is because some herbs, like garlic and ginger, can thin the blood and make bleeding worse. If you're taking medications to control bleeding, drinking herbal teas with these properties could make your bleeding problems worse.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs, like sage and pennyroyal, can cause problems. While peppermint and chamomile teas are often considered safe, it's not clear how they might affect gum infections. To be safe, it's best to choose plain, warm water or sugar-free gum to help manage gum infections during pregnancy.
faq_breastfeeding: Some herbal teas may help with gum infections, but it's a good idea to check the ingredients first. While breastfeeding, it's best to choose teas that are known to be safe for both mom and baby. Tea ingredients like chamomile and peppermint can be a good choice, but always read the labels to be sure.
title_2: Diabetics with Gum Infection: 10 Herbal Creams to Try
